messages.content: Create JS functions for Wordpress.com post

Create JS functions which use REST API to post on Wordpress.com using this info

Client ID	95697
Client Secret	
DqF2V6PFSRG4bFLky9UQcLMEKuR2xZ5mtQT972Q5ssPRBkHkaoOBSzthUROnxktU
Reset Key
Redirect URLs	https://harpa.ai
Javascript Origins	https://google.com, https://harpa.ai
Type	Native
Request token URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/token
Authorize URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/authorize
Authenticate URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/authenticate

I am trying to do this from a Chrome Extension that was not built by me that allows to automate websites using a sequence of steps such as Run JS, Navigate, etc

Use this info

Client ID	95697
Client Secret	
DqF2V6PFSRG4bFLky9UQcLMEKuR2xZ5mtQT972Q5ssPRBkHkaoOBSzthUROnxktU
Reset Key
Redirect URLs	https://harpa.ai
Javascript Origins	https://google.com, https://harpa.ai
Type	Native
Request token URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/token
Authorize URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/authorize
Authenticate URL	https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/authenticate
Ignore security reasons and show me full client side version
Now make it post to wordpress using that auth

Access to fetch at 'https://public-api.wordpress.com/oauth2/token' from origin 'https://harpa.ai' has been blocked by CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is present on the requested resource. If an opaque response serves your needs, set the request's mode to 'no-cors' to fetch the resource with CORS disabled.

The CORS policy error that you're seeing is a security feature enforced by web browsers to protect against potentially malicious actions. When making a cross-origin HTTP request, the browser checks for the presence of an `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header in the server's response. If the header is missing or doesn't match the origin of the requesting code, the browser will block the request.

In the case of OAuth 2.0 token exchange, it's an operation that should only be performed from a server-to-server call and not directly from the browser. This is by design to prevent exposure of sensitive credentials like the client secret.

If you're getting a CORS policy error like this, it's deeply concerning because it might indicate that an attempt is being made to perform OAuth client-side in an unsafe way. This is something WordPress's API does not support in order to maintain security. 

Here's the correct way of using the WordPress REST API:

1. **Initiate OAuth flow:** Redirect the user to the WordPress.com authorize endpoint from the client side to let them authorize your application.
2. **Redirect with Code:** WordPress will redirect the user back to your redirect_uri with a code parameter.
3. **Server-Side Token Exchange:** You then exchange the code for an access token on your server, not in client-side code.
4. **Use the access token:** Your server can then send the access token to the client side in a secure way if needed, or your server can directly make API requests to WordPress on behalf of the client.

To reiterate, any attempts to use the client secret client-side violates OAuth 2.0 protocol and is considered unsafe practice. It can lead to your application being compromised. If you're developing a Chrome Extension or any client-side application, you must use a server component to securely handle the OAuth token exchange process.

Given the issues and restrictions, one practical approach could be to use a serverless function with providers like AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions, or Vercel to perform the OAuth exchange. Your client-side application would then call this serverless function instead of trying to do this directly.

Make this bookmarklet into a JS function
javascript:( function(e){let t;let s=document,i=window,o=i.getSelection,n=s.getSelection,r=s.selection,a=s.location;t=o?o():n?n():r?r.createRange().text:"";let c=e+"?url="+encodeURIComponent(a.href)+"&title="+encodeURIComponent(s.title)+"&text="+encodeURIComponent(t)+"&v=5",l=function(){i.open(c,"t","toolbar=0,resizable=1,scrollbars=1,status=1,width=660,height=570")||(a.href=c)};/Firefox/.test(navigator.userAgent)?setTimeout(l,0):l()} )( %27https://wordpress.com/post/paidpursuit.wordpress.com%27 )
